title: Coral Bleaching Detection And Reef Recovery Tracking In The Great Barrier Reef
summary: Build a verified workflow for satellite-based coral bleaching detection and reef recovery tracking in the Great Barrier Reef, linking thermal stress data to observed bleaching severity and subsequent recovery or mortality.
why_it_matters: The Great Barrier Reef has experienced six mass bleaching events since 1998, with 2024 marking the fifth in eight years. Coral bleaching driven by marine heatwaves threatens Australia's $6 billion reef tourism industry, biodiversity, and coastal protection. Satellite SST data can detect bleaching conditions, but linking thermal stress to actual bleaching severity and tracking recovery requires in-water validation that is spatially sparse.

success_metrics.target: Satellite bleaching alerts validated against in-water surveys.
success_metrics.validation_method: Compare NOAA CRW alerts to AIMS survey data.

success_metrics.target: Recovery classified as full, partial, or none with time-since-bleaching documented.
No reef-management recommendation merged without review and replication.
success_metrics.validation_method: Check task status and review records.

review_policy.merge_gate: Schema validation, domain review, safety review, replication.
